title: Fix Staff Management Page crash on 'Add Staff'

requirements.summary: Fix the crash/error in AddEmployeePage.tsx that occurs when clicking 'Add Staff' button
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Add Staff' on the HR Employees page opens the AddEmployeePage form without any crash or error screen.
The Direct Manager dropdown populates with active manager-role employees without throwing a runtime error.
All form fields (name, email, department, role, username, password, directManagerId, etc.) are bound correctly and accept user input.
Submitting the form with valid data calls the backend employee creation endpoint without an unhandled exception.
Inline validation errors are shown for empty required fields without crashing.
A success toast is displayed and the user is navigated back to the employee list on successful submission.
No TypeScript compilation errors exist in AddEmployeePage.tsx.

file_operations.description: Fix all runtime errors in the AddEmployeePage component: Guard against null/undefined actor access by checking the actor exists before rendering the form; fix type mismatches on the directManagerId field by ensuring it is properly typed as optional string and the manager dropdown correctly handles the field binding; ensure all form fields (fullName, email, department, jobTitle, phone, role, badgeId, startDate, directManagerId, username, password) are properly bound to form state with correct TypeScript types matching the CreateEmployeeWithAccountInput interface; wrap the mutation call in proper error handling to catch and display backend errors without crashing; add loading state checks to prevent form submission while the mutation is in progress; ensure the Direct Manager dropdown filters employees by role (manager, supervisor, hrAdmin) and status (active) and maps them correctly to dropdown options; verify form validation triggers inline error messages without throwing exceptions; ensure navigation back to the employees list occurs only after successful submission.

requirements.summary: Audit and fix the mutation hook in useQueries.ts for employee creation
acceptanceCriteria: The mutation hook does not throw if the actor is null or undefined — it rejects with a descriptive error message instead.
A backend #ok response resolves the Promise and triggers employees query cache invalidation.
A backend #err response rejects the Promise with the error text, which is surfaced to the form as an inline error.
No unhandled promise rejections occur during the mutation call.

file_operations.description: Fix the useCreateEmployeeWithAccount mutation hook (or equivalent employee creation mutation) to guard against null/undefined actor by checking if actor exists before calling the backend method and throwing a descriptive error if not; ensure the mutationFn properly awaits the backend call and handles the response (the backend method createEmployeeWithAccount returns Promise<void>, so verify it resolves correctly); add proper error handling to catch any exceptions from the backend call and reject the Promise with a user-friendly error message; configure the onSuccess callback to invalidate the 'employees' query key using queryClient.invalidateQueries so the employee list refreshes automatically after successful creation; ensure the mutation hook is properly typed with CreateEmployeeWithAccountInput as the input parameter type.
